nickel-wasm is our fork. you can check the upstream, unmodded nickel code in ../nickel/

what about this? This is the real root cause: import resolution creates shared thunks
 in the CacheHub. When a Nickel file is imported multiple times (or
 imported once but accessed through multiple evaluation paths), the
 import resolves to a single CacheIndex. If path A is mid-forcing
 that import when path B also needs it, blackhole.

 The durable fix: the WASM bridge should pre-resolve and cache
 imports before evaluation starts, so no import thunks remain to
 blackhole. Or equivalently: the shim should avoid multiple paths
 into the same import.

 But actually, the simplest fix that addresses the real architectural
 issue: stop routing through onix.ncl. The shim already knows which
 library modules each consumer needs. Instead of passing the entire
 onix bundle, pass individual imports directly.
